obligations.reason: apb_soc_ctrl exposes security-sensitive SoC execution and cluster-control outputs through an APB register interface; visible facts show no requester privilege metadata on the APB ports, so the RTL must be checked for any internal or upstream guard before allowing writes.
obligations.subject: APB requester reaching soc_peripherals/s_soc_ctrl_bus
obligations.object: fc_bootaddr_o, fc_fetchen_o, cluster_boot_addr_o, cluster_fetch_enable_o, cluster_rstn_o, cluster_pow_o, cluster_byp_o, sel_hyper_axi_o, cluster_irq_o
obligations.expected_guard: Writes to SoC control registers that change boot address, fetch enable, cluster reset/power/bypass, IRQ, and AXI route selection should be mediated by a privilege/lifecycle/debug-authorization or equivalent access-control condition beyond raw APB decode.
obligations.candidate_path: pulp_soc APB/peripheral interconnect -> soc_peripherals -> apb_soc_ctrl APB registers -> boot/fetch/power/reset outputs
state_conditions_to_check: whether controls can be changed after boot/fetch enable is asserted
whether any lock/sticky bit exists and protects all write paths
required_evidence_slots: subject/requester APB master or bus path
expected guard signal or absence at write site
obligations.uncertainty: Fact layer is truncated and does not include full internal register decode; obligation is to inspect visible RTL.

obligations.reason: The top-level facts expose s_lint_debug_bus, s_lint_jtag_bus, s_apb_debug_bus, and an i_lint_jtag instance; debug paths are common bypass candidates but require RTL evidence of reachability and missing authorization.
obligations.subject: JTAG/debug requester
obligations.object: L2/SCM/ROM memory buses and APB peripheral register space reachable from debug/JTAG paths
obligations.expected_guard: Debug/JTAG-originated memory or peripheral accesses should be authorized by debug authentication/lifecycle enable and should preserve address-range and access-policy checks for protected memories/peripherals.
obligations.candidate_path: JTAG/debug bus/interface -> pulp_soc lint_jtag_wrap or s_lint_jtag_bus/s_lint_debug_bus -> SoC interconnect/L2/ROM/peripheral paths
state_conditions_to_check: whether debug can access while CPU/peripheral locks are set
required_evidence_slots: debug/JTAG subject signal and instance connection
authorization/lifecycle/debug-enable guard at entry or use site
obligations.uncertainty: Do not assume vulnerability from debug signal presence; prove reachable protected object and guard behavior.

obligations.reason: apb_fll_if has explicit fll1_lock/fll2_lock/fll3_lock inputs and APB-controlled FLL request/write outputs; inspect whether lock state actually gates all write/configuration paths and cannot be bypassed by address cases or pending transactions.
obligations.subject: APB requester reaching FLL interface
obligations.object: fll1_req/fll1_wrn/fll1_add/fll1_data, fll2_*, fll3_* frequency/clock configuration interface
obligations.expected_guard: Writes/configuration updates to FLL control/status registers should be blocked or constrained when corresponding fll*_lock indicates locked state, and readback of lock/status should not create an unsafe control bypass.
obligations.candidate_path: APB bus -> apb_fll_if -> fll1/fll2/fll3 request/write/read ports and lock inputs
required_evidence_slots: read path PRDATA for FLL registers
obligations.uncertainty: The lock semantics must be inferred from RTL use, not from port names alone.

obligations.reason: The design uses APB node/wrapper address routing to multiple security-relevant peripherals; permission failures can arise from aliasing or default routing that exposes protected registers to unintended address regions.
obligations.subject: Any APB master on peripheral bus
obligations.object: APB slave select signals for soc_ctrl, FLL, GPIO, uDMA, timers, stdout, event generator and protected configuration registers
obligations.expected_guard: Peripheral address decoding should enforce non-overlapping ranges, generate PSEL only for intended slaves, and return errors/no access for unmapped or out-of-range addresses without aliasing protected registers.
obligations.uncertainty: Need exact constants and decode code from includes/RTL.

obligations.reason: Top-level facts show uDMA APB configuration bus and lint uDMA TX/RX buses; DMA engines can act as independent bus masters and require range/permission mediation distinct from CPU access.
obligations.subject: uDMA TX/RX engine
obligations.object: L2/SCM/ROM or peripheral address spaces reachable by uDMA TX/RX bus paths
obligations.expected_guard: uDMA-generated read/write transfers should be constrained by address range checks or configuration permissions, and configuration updates should not alter active transfer addresses/lengths to bypass intended bounds.
obligations.candidate_path: uDMA subsystem master ports -> SoC interconnect/L2/peripheral/memory regions
obligations.uncertainty: Internal uDMA channel details not visible in facts; inspect module body and connections.

obligations.reason: GPIO registers are externally observable/controlling; if any GPIO pins are security-sensitive in this SoC scope, APB writes/reads require appropriate mediation. Obligation focuses on checking visible RTL paths and pin use, not assuming all GPIOs are protected.
obligations.subject: APB requester reaching GPIO
obligations.object: gpio_out, gpio_dir, gpio_in/gpio_in_sync, interrupt configuration registers
obligations.expected_guard: GPIO direction/output/interrupt control that can affect board-level security signals should be protected by pin policy, lock, or privilege guard where pins are designated sensitive; reads of gpio_in should not expose protected strap/debug/lifecycle pins without mediation.
obligations.candidate_path: APB bus -> apb_gpio -> gpio_dir/gpio_out/interrupt configuration and external pins
state_conditions_to_check: ability to change direction after boot/lifecycle
interrupt trigger configuration on sensitive pins
required_evidence_slots: GPIO APB address decode and register writes
pin mapping to SoC top-level or sensitive functions
obligations.uncertainty: Sensitivity depends on top-level pin mapping; verify before escalating.

obligations.reason: Timer peripherals can generate SoC events/IRQs and external channel outputs from APB-programmed state; inspect whether any protected interrupt/event routing requires guards beyond raw APB access.
obligations.subject: APB requester and external timer event inputs
obligations.object: events_o, ch_0_o..ch_3_o, irq_lo_o, irq_hi_o, timer compare/count/config registers
obligations.expected_guard: Timer/event generator configuration capable of triggering interrupts, events, or output waveforms should be restricted if used for privileged control, and event inputs should not allow untrusted external events to override protected timer state without enable/policy checks.
state_conditions_to_check: pending event/IRQ after reset or disable
external event input while timer disabled
required_evidence_slots: APB write decode for timer control
obligations.uncertainty: Only security-relevant if connected to privileged event/interrupt effects; check SoC connections.

obligations.reason: Clock/reset controls can disable or perturb protected domains; visible APB clock divider and SoC control modules should be checked for permission gating and safe transition conditions.
obligations.subject: APB requester reaching clock/reset control registers
obligations.object: clk_div0/1/2, clk_div*_valid, sel_fll_clk_i related controls, reset/clock-domain control outputs
obligations.expected_guard: Clock divider, clock selection, and reset-generation control registers should require privilege/lifecycle guard or safe-state checks before changing clock/reset behavior for protected domains.
obligations.candidate_path: APB bus -> apb_clkdiv and clock/reset generator controls -> clock dividers/valids and reset/clock selection outputs
required_evidence_slots: APB register write sites for dividers/control
obligations.uncertainty: Need module internals and clock/reset generator connections to decide security impact.

obligations.reason: The interface definitions include test/BIST and routing/control signals that can affect memory/core behavior; inspect whether any normal-mode APB/debug path can manipulate them without test/lifecycle authorization.
obligations.subject: APB/debug/test requester or configuration driver
obligations.object: bist_en/bist_pattern/result, bbmux_* selects, clkgate_* selects, mmu_sram_seqsec_size, mmu_scm_seqsec_size
obligations.expected_guard: Test/BIST, mux, clock-gate, and MMU size/control signals should be driven only by authorized configuration or lifecycle/test mode and should not be writable or controllable by untrusted APB/debug paths in normal operation.
obligations.candidate_path: pulp_interfaces test/BIST/MMU/clock-gate/bbmux interfaces -> SoC memory/core routing controls
state_conditions_to_check: normal operation with test mode disabled
sticky lock or one-way lifecycle behavior
obligations.uncertainty: pulp_interfaces is mostly interface definitions; need connected drivers/use sites.

obligations.reason: Multiple masters are visible (FC data/instr, debug, JTAG, uDMA) and multiple protected regions exist; check for inconsistent decode, missing requester-based restrictions, and read/write/fetch policy differences.
obligations.subject: FC data/instruction, debug/JTAG, uDMA masters
obligations.object: s_mem_rom_bus, s_scm_l2_data_bus, s_scm_l2_instr_bus, peripheral bus, L2/SCM/ROM target regions
obligations.expected_guard: Interconnect should preserve requester identity/security attributes or otherwise enforce address-range policy consistently for instruction/data/debug/DMA/JTAG masters accessing protected memory and peripheral regions.
obligations.candidate_path: SoC interconnect and wrappers -> ROM/L2/SCM memory buses and peripheral bus bridges
required_evidence_slots: list of masters and target ports
path from unauthorized requester to protected target
obligations.uncertainty: The visible facts do not show a privilege signal; determine whether policy is intended in RTL or external system.
